answer:
**The third couplet (*sher*) of the ghazal in *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, chapter 5, is:**

> Chaman afroz hai sayyaad meri khush-nawaai tak  
> Rahi bijli ki be-taabi, so mere aashiyaan tak hai[^1]

This is from the transliteration variant (as the query title matches this script). The chapter is a 9-couplet ghazal where each *sher* follows the refrain "tak hai," expressing themes of futile longing (*be-dili*) and inner turmoil.[^2]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch.5 ("Kahoon kya aarzoo-e be-dili mujh ko kahan tak hai"), [transliteration](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/05-kahoon-kya-aarzoo-e-be-dili-mujh-ko-kahan-tak-hai/transliteration/).

[^2]: Ibid., wiki card (key passages and structure).
